steps.title: Identify the contradiction in the classification
steps.content: The passage establishes thalassocracies (sea-based) and tellurocracies (land-based) as distinct categories. The Ottoman Empire then contradicts this neat binary by having both land and sea supremacy. The transition must acknowledge this exception.

steps.title: Match the transition to the contradiction
steps.content: Choice D (“Of course, the distinction is not always so neat”) directly cues an exception: the Ottoman Empire has both land and sea control. Choices A, B, and C all reinforce the classification system instead. That matches choice D — D is correct.
